entry.title: How AI and Machine Learning Are Quietly Transforming SAP Integrations — My Perspective as a Beginner
entry.content:
The Gap I Noticed in Traditional Integration
In a typical iFlow I built recently, I had to manually define rules like:
∙ If amount is greater than 100000 — route to high value channel
∙ If region is not IN or US — mark as invalid
∙ If quantity is zero — reject the item
These rules work fine when business logic is simple and stable. But what happens when the rules keep changing? What if the threshold for “high value” changes every quarter? What if new regions get added every month? A human has to go in, update the Groovy script, redeploy, test again. That is time consuming and error prone.
This is exactly where AI and ML start making sense in SAP integrations.

 

Where AI/ML Actually Fits in SAP

1. Intelligent Data Mapping

“Mail Based Batch Processing iFlow — routing logic manually defined for each invoice type” 

One of the most frustrating parts of SAP CPI for a beginner is Message Mapping. You have to manually connect every source field to every target field. For simple scenarios this is manageable. But imagine mapping two enterprise systems with hundreds of fields each.
SAP has been working on AI assisted mapping where the system suggests field mappings based on field names, data types and historical mapping patterns. Instead of manually dragging every line, the AI proposes the mapping and you just review and approve. This saves enormous time in large integration projects.

This is my data in Postman


2. Anomaly Detection in Integration Flows

“Manually setting threshold values in Content Modifier — AI could dynamically adjust these thresholds based on patterns”

Imagine your iFlow processes 10000 invoices every day. Suddenly one day 200 invoices fail. Is that normal? Is something broken? Did a source system change its format?
ML models trained on historical message processing logs can detect these anomalies automatically. Instead of waiting for someone to notice failures in the monitor, the system alerts you proactively. SAP Integration Advisor and SAP Alert Notification Service are already moving in this direction.


3. Predictive Error Handling

“Manually defined routing conditions in SAP CPI Router — AI could learn and suggest these rules automatically based on historical data”

Traditional error handling in CPI is reactive — something fails, you get an alert, you investigate. ML changes this to proactive. By analyzing patterns in past failures — which steps fail most often, at what times, with what kind of payloads — a model can predict which messages are likely to fail before they actually do and reroute them automatically.


4. Natural Language to iFlow

“Custom business logic written manually in Groovy — imagine describing this in plain English and having AI generate it automatically” 

This one genuinely surprised me when I read about it. Researchers and SAP teams are experimenting with systems where you describe what you want in plain English — something like “receive invoices from vendor system, validate amounts, send high value ones to finance team via email” — and the system automatically generates a working iFlow.
We are not fully there yet but with large language models becoming more capable this is not science fiction anymore.


5. AI Powered Document Understanding
In my Scenario 3 assignment I processed invoices that were already structured XML. But in the real world invoices come as PDFs, scanned images, emails and all kinds of unstructured formats. SAP Document Information Extraction — part of SAP AI Business Services — uses ML to read these unstructured documents and extract fields like invoice number, vendor name, amount and date automatically. This extracted data can then flow directly into an SAP CPI iFlow for further processing.

 

What SAP Is Actually Building
SAP has been investing heavily in an umbrella platform called SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad. These are the infrastructure layers that allow AI models to be trained, deployed and consumed across SAP systems.
On top of that, specific AI services have been released:
∙ SAP Document Information Extraction — extracts data from unstructured documents
∙ SAP Business Entity Recognition — identifies business entities in text
∙ SAP Translation Hub — ML powered translation for global deployments
∙ SAP Intelligent Robotic Process Automation — combines RPA with ML for smarter automation
All of these can be connected to SAP Integration Suite, meaning your iFlows can call these AI services as external APIs and enrich your integration logic with intelligence.

A Small Real World Example
Let me paint a picture. Suppose a company receives invoices from 50 different vendors. Each vendor sends invoices in a slightly different format — different field names, different structures, different currencies.
Without AI:
∙ A developer manually creates one mapping per vendor
∙ 50 vendors means 50 mappings to maintain
∙ Every time a vendor changes their format someone has to update the mapping
With AI:
∙ ML model learns the pattern of each vendor’s format from historical data
∙ Automatically adapts mappings when formats change slightly
∙ Flags only major structural changes for human review
∙ Developer effort reduces from weeks to hours

 

My Honest Take


I am still a beginner with SAP CPI. I am still figuring out XPath expressions and Groovy scripts. But even at this level I can see that the manual rule based approach has limits. The more I work with integration flows the more I appreciate why AI is being brought into this space.
The integrations of tomorrow will not just move data — they will understand it, validate it intelligently, predict problems before they happen and adapt to changes automatically. SAP is clearly building towards that future and I think it is genuinely exciting for anyone working in this space today.

References and Further Reading
∙ SAP AI Business Services on SAP Discovery Center
∙ SAP Integration Advisor Documentation
∙ SAP AI Core on SAP Help Portal
∙ SAP Community blogs on Intelligent Integration

 This blog reflects my personal observations and learning while working with SAP Integration Suite as part of my coursework. All scenarios mentioned are based on hands-on assignment work.

entry.title: SAP Joule skill throwing byte_limit_size_exception
entry.content:
Hi.

While building Joule skills that gets data from actions, we run into "byte_limit_size_exception" error at times. Which says that our response size is larger than 10000 bytes. Open search in the internet is showing any references to this error.

We optimise the inputs and outputs as much as possible, but sometimes, its near impossible to compress outputs further.

Is this a hard limit? or is it applicable only for testing? Can this be changed?

Thank you

entry.title: Custom Adobe Forms in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud using Developer Extensibility
entry.content:
Introduction

  • Custom Adobe forms can be created in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud using Developer Extensibility using Form Objects.
  • OData Service can be created to get Form details.

Process to Develop Custom Adobe Forms in SAP S4HANA Public Cloud using Developer Extensibility

Forms Objects

  • Creating Forms Objects in the Form editor allows for the generation of print and interactive forms using Adobe Document Services.
  • Forms Objects can be created in the Form editor.
  • Print and interactive forms can be generated using Adobe Document Services (ADS) by the Forms Object.
  • The main part of Forms Object is layout form design file (XDP format by Adobe).
  • Adobe LiveCycle Designer is required for the development of XDP layout file.

Creating Form Objects

  • A Form Object can be created in ABAP development tools for Eclipse (ADT) using the creation wizard

Print Forms

ADS Rendering in ABAP environment

  • In the ABAP environment with Adobe Document Services (ADS), rendering refers to the process of taking an Adobe XML Form (XFA) which is essentially a structured template containing layout, design, and data placeholders and producing a final output document.
  • The Adobe Document Services (ADS) can render Adobe XML Forms (XFA) to PDF documents and other print formats.
  • The application sends the data and the form template using a secure connection and the service returns the rendered document.
  • The rendered documents can be used by the applications for the screen output or for the output into the print queue.

Retrieve Stored Form Templates

Form templates designed with the Adobe LiveCycle Designer can be uploaded directly to the system using ABAP development tools for Eclipse.

The class CL_FP_FORM_READER enables the usage of uploaded form templates during runtime.

Initiate the Form Template Reader

Initiate the class by providing the name of the uploaded form template.

data(lo_reader) = cl_fp_form_reader=>create_form_reader( 'MY_FORM_NAME' ).

Read Layout Content to be Used by ADS Rendering Call

The layout can also be retrieved by the reader class.

The GET_LAYOUT method has the returning parameter RV_LAYOUT, it returns XML as XSTRING

RAP Data Services for Print Forms

  • The XML data for print form rendering can be obtained from the RAP Business Services.
  • The data model can be defined just as well as the RAP Service Definition.
  • The CL_FP_FDP_SERVICES class provides the ABAP API to receive the data in xml format for the use in print forms.
  • XML Schema Definition (XSD) used for the design of print forms in the Adobe LiveCycle Designer.

Initiate Business Data Reader

To supply the rendering operation with business data, a reuse service can be applied.

Service definition needs to be created previously.

The service definition doesn't need a binding for RAP Data Services for print forms.

Initiate the reuse utility by specifying the service definition.

DATA(lo_fdp_api) = cl_fp_fdp_services=>get_instance( ‘FP_FDP_SERVICE_EX1’ ).

Specify the key parameters.

This is required to read the data for a specific item.

The reuse utility offers the functionality to retrieve all key fields for the service definition.

DATA(lt_keys)    = lo_fdp_api->get_keys( ).

Once the array with key values has been retrieved, the keys that fit are assigned to the document to be output.

lt_keys[ name = 'UUID' ]-value = '4500000001'.

Retrieve XML Data from Service Definition

The READ_TO_XML_V2 method retrieves the data structure of the service definition for a specific item.

It has the Importing parameters IT_SELECT - Key parameters, IV_LANGUAGE (Optional).

It has the returning parameter RV_XML - Resulting XML as XSTRING.

TRY.
    DATA(lv_data) = lo_fdp_api->read_to_xml_v2( lt_keys ).
CATCH cx_fp_fdp_error INTO DATA(lo_exception).
ENDTRY.

Retrieve XML Schema Definition from Service Definition

The GET_XSD_V2 method retrieves the XML Schema Definition (XSD) for the service definition.

It has the returning parameter RV_XML, it returns XSD as XSTRING.

TRY.
   DATA(lv_xml)     = lo_fdp_api->get_xsd_v2(  ).
CATCH cx_fp_fdp_error INTO DATA(lo_exception).
ENDTRY

Runtime API for ADS Rendering Calls

The CL_FP_ADS_UTIL class provides the ABAP Runtime API for Adobe Document Services (ADS) rendering calls.

It contains the public method RENDER_PDF to be used for the Rendering PDF.

The PDF rendering process on ADS creates the PDF file to be printed (or print output format) using both formats

  • XDP form template file
  • Data for the form filling in XML format, for example, data from the RAP Data Services for Print Forms.

The XDP form template file is a description of the form layout design in Adobe XFA format.

The supported data binding type is an XML data schema.

The data XML file contains the data that corresponds to the used data schema in the XDP form template.

Rendering PDF

The RENDER_PDF method calls the ADS to render print PDF using the XDP form file and the XML data file. 

TRY.

*Render PDF
    cl_fp_ads_util=>render_pdf(
                             EXPORTING
                                   iv_xml_data      = lv_xml_data
                                   iv_xdp_layout    = lv_xdp
                                   iv_locale        = 'de_DE'
                                   is_options       = ls_options
                             IMPORTING
                                   ev_pdf           = ev_pdf
                                   ev_pages         = ev_pages
                                  ev_trace_string  = ev_trace_string ).
  CATCH cx_fp_ads_util INTO lx_fp_ads_util.
ENDTRY.

Importing Parameters

IV_XML_DATA - XML data

IV_XDP_LAYOUT - Adobe XDP form template

IV_LOCALE - Locale for rendering the language language_COUNTRY, (en_US)

IS_OPTIONS - PDF rendering parameters (optional)

Exporting Parameters

EV_PDF - PDF rendering result

EV_PAGES - Pages

EV_TRACE_STRING - Trace string

Creating Data Provider

Create Data Model

Create a data model, add the annotation to the CDS View to act a data provider for FORM Object.

      @ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities: [ #OUTPUT_FORM_DATA_PROVIDER ]

            supportedCapabilities defines which features or capabilities are supported by the CDS view or entity.

           #OUTPUT_FORM_DATA_PROVIDER is a specific capability flag that indicates the CDS entity can act as a data provider for output forms.

CDS View – ZI_FORM_PO_HD

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Form PO'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities: [ #OUTPUT_FORM_DATA_PROVIDER ]
define root view entity ZI_FORM_PO_HD
  as select from ZI_PO_HD_DT
{
  key PoNum,
      Supplier,
      PurOrg,
      CompCod,
      PurGrp,
      CrDt,
      _its
}

Data Source CDS View – ZI_PO_HD_DT

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'PO Header'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define root view entity ZI_PO_HD_DT
  as select from ztb_po_hdr
  association [0..*] to ZI_PO_ITS as _its on $projection.PoNum = _its.PoNum
{
  key po_num   as PoNum,
      supplier as Supplier,
      pur_org  as PurOrg,
      comp_cod as CompCod,
      pur_grp  as PurGrp,
      cr_dt    as CrDt,
      _its
}

Custom Table

@EndUserText.label : 'PO Header'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #ALLOWED
define table ztb_po_hdr {

  key client : abap.clnt not null;
  key po_num : abap.char(10) not null;
  supplier   : abap.char(10);
  pur_org    : abap.char(5);
  comp_cod   : abap.char(5);
  pur_grp    : abap.char(5);
  cr_dt      : abap.dats;

}

Create Service Definition

For a service definition, a leading entity can be defined using annotation

       @ObjectModel.leadingEntity.name: ''

  Add the CDS View as a leading entity.

@EndUserText.label: 'Service Definition of PO Form'
@ObjectModel.leadingEntity.name: 'ZI_FORM_PO_HD'
define service ZSR_FORM_PO_HD {
  expose ZI_FORM_PO_HD;
}

Service Definition ZSR_FORM_PO_HD is a Data provider for Form Object.

Service Binding

The service definition doesn't need a binding for RAP Data Services for print forms.

Creating Form Object

Create a Form Object with creation wizard from the main menu, toolbar, Project Explorer, or using a shortcut.

Enter the name and description of a form.

Select Next, assign a transport request, and select finish.

A new form is created and opened in the Form Editor.

A Data Provider now needs to be specified, which is defined in the system as a service containing the business data for this form.

In the General Information section, define the properties of the form

  • In the Data provider field, specify the service definition that should be used for fetching the business data for a form.
  • The Embed Font field should be enabled if the fonts are to be embedded into the generated PDF.
  • The Reduce Data Volume field should be enabled if the generated business data is to be filtered to only contain fields bound by fields in the form layout.

Choose Browse option to view the list of Data Providers.

Added the Data Provider ZSR_FORM_PO_HD to the Form

In the action menu, three options

  • Download Schema: Download the schema of the linked Data provider as an xsd file which can be imported into Adobe LiveCycle Designer for SAP solutions.
  • Upload Layout: Upload a layout file (*.xdp) created by Adobe LiveCycle Designer for SAP solutions (make sure the form should save before uploading a layout file).
  • Download Layout: Download the current layout uploaded to the form.

Choose Download Schema to create a new layout for the form.

Export and save schema to local files.

Open the Adobe Life Cycle Designer

Create a new blank form

Blank Form is created

Choose File -> New Data Connection

Choose XML Schema

Import the XML Schema File

Data Connection is added.

Design the layout

Save the layout file (XDP file) to local files.

Upload the Layout to the Form Object

Layout is uploaded, save the form object

Activate the Form Object

Layout can be downloaded with Download Layout option

Consuming the Form using Custom Entity

Custom Entity

Create a custom entity, add the form related fields and add the annotation to make its content as attachment.

@Semantics.largeObject:{ mimeType: 'MimyType', fileName: 'FileName',         
                         contentDispositionPreference: #ATTACHMENT }

Add the ABAP Class to implement the form access.

@EndUserText.label: 'PO Form'
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_FORM_PO'
define custom entity ZI_FORM_PO

{
  key PO       : ebeln;
      @Semantics.largeObject:{ mimeType: 'MimyType', fileName: 'FileName', contentDispositionPreference: #ATTACHMENT }
      Form     : abap.rawstring( 0 );
      FileName : abap.char( 30 );
      MimyType : abap.char( 50 );
}

Class- ZCL_FORM_PO

CLASS zcl_form_po DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_rap_query_provider. 
PROTECTED SECTION.
PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS

CLASS zcl_form_po IMPLEMENTATION.

 METHOD if_rep_query_provider~select
     
      DATA: 1s_form TYPE zi_form_po,
            it_form TYPE TABLE OF zi_form_po.

      TRY.

           DATA(it_filter_conds) = io_request->get_filter()->get_as_ranges(). 
           LOOP AT it_filter_conds INTO DATA(1s_filter_conds).
            IF ls_filter_conds-name EQ 'PO'.
              READ TABLE ls_filter_conds-range INTO DATA(ls_rng) INDEX 1.
              IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
               DATA(lv_po) = ls_rng-low.
              ENDIF.
            ENDIF
            CLEAR: ls_rng.
           ENDLOOP.

         DATA(lv_paging) = io_request->get_paging( )->get_offset( ).
         DATA(lv_filters) = io_request->get_filter( )->get_as_sql_string( ).

       CATCH cx_rap_query_filter_no_range INTO DATA(cx_mg).

       IF 1v_po 15 INITIAL.
        lv_po = '4500000001'.
       ENDIF.

      TRY.
       "Initiate the Fore Template Reader
        DATA(lo_reader) = cl_fp_form_reader->create_form_reader( iv_formname = 'ZFORM_PO' ).
       "Read Layout Content to be Used by ADS Rendering Call 
        DATA(lv_layout) = lo_reader-get_layout().
      CATCH cx_fp_form_reader INTO DATA(ls_cx_fp_form_reader). 
      ENDTRY.

      TRY.
      "Get instance of Form
      DATA(lo_fdp_api) = cl_fdp_services->get instance(
                                  iv service definition = 'ZSR_FORM_PO_HD'
                                "  iv root node =
                                  iv_max_depth = 1 ).

      "Retrieve x Data from Service Definition
       DATA(ls_keys) = lo_fdp_api->get_keys( ).
       it_keys[ name = 'PONUM' ]->value = lv_po.
       
       DATA(lv_data) = lo_fdp_api->read_to_xml_v2( 
                                   it select = it_keys
                                  "it_select_add = 
                                  "iv_language = SY-LANGU 
                                     ).

       "Retrive XML Schema Definition from Definition
        DATA(lv_xml) = lo_fdp_api->get_xsd_v2( ).

      CATCH cx_fp_fdp_error INTO DATA(lo_exception).
     ENDTRY.

    TRY.
   "Rendering PDF
    cl_fp_ads_util=>render_pdf(
        EXPORTING
          iv_xml_data = lv_data   
          iv_xdp_layout = lv_layout
          iv_locale  = 'en_US'
       "   i_options = 
         IMPORTING
           ev_pdf = DATA(ev_pdf)
         ev_pages = 
         ev_trace_string =  
        ).
   
 CATCH cx_fp_ads_util INTO DATA(lo_fp_ads_util).

ENDTRY.

ls_form-po = lv_po.
ls_form-filename = 'Form_PO.pdf'.
ls_form-mimytype = 'application/pdf'.
ls_form-form = ev_pdf.
APPEND ls_form TO it_form.

io_response->set_data( it_form ).
io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lines( it_form ) ).

ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Service Definition

Create a Service Definition, expose the entity.

@EndUserText.label: 'FORM_PO'
define service ZSD_FORM_PO {
  expose ZI_FORM_PO;
}

Service Binding – Web API

Create a Service Binding with type OData V4 – Web API

Click on Service URL to access it.

Service URL

Access form details

Download Form with URL

Download the Form with accessing the service URL by passing document number.

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zsrb_form_po/srvd_a2x/sap/zsd_form_po/0001/ZI_FORM_PO('4500000001')/Form

Form is downloaded

Service Binding – UI

Create a Service Binding with type OData V4 – UI

Download Form with UI

Choose Preview in service binding to view UI

Click on Form to download the Form

Form is downloaded

Debugging the Form Logic

Toggle Breakpoint is set

Click on Form to trigger the breakpoint.

Debugger got triggered.

Variables with values

Using CDS Views

CDS View

Create a CDS View with field having URL of Form

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'PO Header'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define root view entity ZI_PO_HD
  as select from ztb_po_hdr

  association [0..*] to ZI_PO_ITS as _its on $projection.PoNum = _its.PoNum
{
  key po_num                                                                                                               as PoNum,
      supplier                                                                                                             as Supplier,
      pur_org                                                                                                              as PurOrg,
      comp_cod                                                                                                             as CompCod,
      pur_grp                                                                                                              as PurGrp,
      cr_dt                                                                                                                as CrDt,

      //      cast( 'PO Form.pdf' as abap.char( 20 ))                                                                              as FileName,
      //
      //      cast( 'application/pdf' as abap.char( 30 ))                                                                          as MimeType,

      concat( '/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zsb_form_po/srvd_a2x/sap/zsd_form_po/0001/ZI_FORM_PO(''', concat( po_num, ''')/Form' )) as FormURL,

      'Print PDF'                                                                                                          as Form,

      _its


}

Metadata Extension

Create metadata extension, add the fields with annotations and add the parameter type with #WITH_URL value, also url with value contains url field name

@Metadata.layer: #CORE
annotate entity ZI_PO_HD with
{
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 10, label: 'PO' )]
@UI.identification: [{ position: 10, label: 'PO' }]
PoNum;

@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 40, label: 'Form URL', type: WITH URL, url:'FormURL' }} 
@UI.identification: [{ position: 40, label: 'Form URL', type: WITH URL, url: 'FormURL' }]
FormURL;

@UI.lineItem: [{position: 30,type: WITH URL, url: 'FormURL' }]
@UI.identification: [{ position: 30, type: #WITH URL, url: 'FormURL' }]
Form;
}

Service Definition

Create a Service Definition, expose the entity.

@EndUserText.label: 'PO Hdr'
define service ZSR_POHDR_PR {
  expose ZI_PO_HD;
}

Service Binding – UI

Create a Service Binding with type OData V4 – UI

Download Form with UI

Choose Preview in service binding to view UI

Click on Print PDF to download the Form

Form is downloaded

Click on Form URL to download the Form

Form is downloaded

References

  • https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/print-forms
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/retrieve-stored-form-templates
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/runtime-api-for-ads-rendering-calls
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/rap-data-services-for-print-forms
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/working-with-forms
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/creating-forms
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/editing-forms

Conclusion

Steps included to develop Custom Adobe Form in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud using Developer Extensibility

  • Creating Data Provider
  • Creating Form Objects
  • Consume the Form using Custom Entity

entry.title: Re: How to create analytical query from custom CDS view
entry.content: Very helpful! Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP PAPM - Disable "Erase data" for End users
entry.content: Hi @Paulo_Moncada, Thank you for your suggestion — really appreciate it. To clarify the requirement: We do not want the end user to be able to erase any data. Instead, the user should only be able to edit specific fields, such as the adjustment value. All other fields — for example Entity, Period, and the calculated amount — must remain intact and should not be modified by the user. We already have the Data Privilege feature enabled for this use case. However, the business need is that each end user, responsible for a specific entity, should be able to apply adjustments on top of the system‑calculated results, without the ability to overwrite or remove the underlying calculated data.

entry.title: Unable to see S userID for Login into SAPforMe
entry.content:
The user's S userID recently expired and when we re-enabled it within the system she no longer has the option to log into me.sap.com with her S userID, it does not show up for her. entry.title: Re: Unable to see S userID for Login into SAPforMe
entry.content:
Regarding to your issue, there are two ways to resolve it, kindly choose one according to your needs.

1. Please refer to KBA 3656247 - Issue with automatic S-user selection during login - SAP Universal ID (UID) as below steps:

1. Access SAP ID Service.
2. Click 'Log Out'.
3. Log in again using the desired S-user ID. 
4. After successfully changing, the updated S-user will appear first on the subsequent page. 

This way will change the default logon S-user of SAP for Me. If you want to change it, follow the above steps to swift the logon S-user.

2. Make sure all the users link with email are same as per KBA 3722976 - User list fails to pop up when attempting to logon using an email address - SAP for Me 

There will be a user list pop-up when you use your email to logon then you should be able to elect the desired S-user to logon.

entry.title: Re: EBS - Processing Rules overlap | SAP S/4HANA Cloud Pu...
entry.content:
Good day @Luca_C 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, as you have noticed, there is a limitation here. If in Public Cloud, one bank statement item matches more than one processing rule at the same time, SAP does not choose the more specific one. Instead, it applies none of those processing rules and falls back to the posting rule that you maintained in Make Global Setting for Electronic Bank Statement. That’s basically the information you got from KBA 3623221.

So, for your scenario, the practical answer to your question: Are there any options to manage this situation?

Answer: No, there is no standard rule priority or “most specific rule wins” logic available for overlapping EBS processing rules.

Your rules must be designed so that they are mutually exclusive. Manage Processing Rules For Bank Statements is a condition-based rule maintenance. It does not provide any mechanism to resolve overlaps between multiple satisfied rules.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/d6fe6526b26f46e59d9d1dd0f7427fe3.html?locale=en-US

 

Here is what I think you should do instead:

  1. Keep the “generic” rule but make it exclusive by adding negative conditions such as Memo Line does not contain the keywords used by the specific rules. This is the direct workaround implied by SAP’s overlap behaviour when creating processing rules.
  2. Make the “specific” rules more selective with additional attributes, no only Memo Line, for example other bank statement item attributes available in the rule conditions. SAP’s rule for framework supports many attributes and operators such contains, does not contain, equal to, and so on.
  3. Redesign th logic so the “generic” rule is no longer generic at the same condition level. In other words, avoid having one broad rule and several narrower rules that can all match at the same item. That is als confirmed in KBA 3623221 as not a viable solution.
  4. To ensure you can maintain those rules, you can use download and upload of processing rules to mass maintain them more easily. Note that automated posting of duplicate or conflicting rules can cause inconsistent data with your overlap idea.

To summarise:

  • Standard priority handling is not available
  • Best practice in Public Cloud is for you to design rules so only one rule can match a bank statement item
  • The most realistic workaround is to use mutually exclusive conditions, which includes terms like “does not contain” in the generic rule, or add more distinguishing attributes to the specific rules

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Status of Perform Further Valuation App Deprecation
entry.content:
Good day @R_Pooja 

Thank you for your question.

SAP has not yet depreciated the Perform Further Valuation app. This will be done in future Public Cloud release.  See SAP’s FAQ for more details.

So, if you recently heard that SAP postponed the depreciation because of identified gaps or user issues, that is consistent with the fact that the current official wording is now generic and therefore does not specify what release the depreciation will be done, rather than a concrete removal version. However, it is not cancelled.

Regarding roadmap visibility, this will be communicated in the SAP Roadmap. You can always contact the following page for any current or future updates. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/9a1d870e446a4062b5f6f3bf50375e84.html?locale=en-US 

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Standard date format issue in Smart Business KPI report
entry.content:
Hi All,

we're facing an issue with Date-Display for CDS-Based Queries in Smart Business KPIs. The date values are not displayed according to user settings (for example dd.mm.yyyy). Instead, the KPI report shows dates in internal format yyyymmdd. Standard format is not working dd.mm.yyyy (yyyymmdd) .

  • In the KPI report (Manage KPIs and Reports), all date fields display as yyyymmdd.
  • The expected behavior is that dates should follow the user’s regional/date settings (e.g., dd.mm.yyyy(yyyymmdd)).

  • In the OData service response, the date dimension contains two fields:

    • PlannedGoodsIssueDate
    • PlannedGoodsIssueDate_T

  • We are not explicitly defining any _T fields in our CDS views, but the _T field still appears in OData.

  • The OData service is using standard generic analytics runtime classes:

    • MPC: CL_NAT_GENERIC_MPC
    • DPC: CL_NAT_GENERIC_DPC

  • When I inspect the Fiori KPI app network calls, the GET request uses $select, but it does not include:

    • PlannedGoodsIssueDate_T

    So the KPI report only fetches PlannedGoodsIssueDate and the _T field never reaches the UI.

 

  1. What is the recommended way to ensure date fields are displayed in the user’s date format in KPI tiles?
  2. Is there any known configuration / best practice / SAP note to address this issue?

Any guidance would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance,

PA

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting Manual JE Not Picked in 3A Investment...
entry.content:
Hello Navin,

The source data for automatic postings for consolidation of investments does not include group dependent postings (posting level 30).

Your journal entry is posted on document type 32 (postsing level 30) with the template 03 Group Dependent Adjustment.

You must post a journal entry on posting level 10 (typically a document type id that begins with 1) using the template 01 Manual Correction

Hope this helps.

Best regards,

Grégoire

entry.title: Re: GROUP REPORTING FIRST CONSOLIDATION
entry.content:
Hi D2Div,

There is not one single direct answer to this large question. It depends on the customer requirements, on the available legacy data in accounting, in consolidation...

In most cases it is recommended to re do the consolidation of the last period of the year preceding the first live consolidation. This is because we need to have opening consolidated balances in the new system that matches the closing balance (Y-1) in the legacy system.

Some customers want also to do parallel runs to get used to the system and check the results with the legacy system.

For loading data from subsidiaries you can use GRDC or import a file directly in Group Reporting. It depends where and how is the source data.

In the FAQ note for group reporting https://me.sap.com/notes/2659656/E you will find attached a document How to Recover Historical Data from SAP Financial Consolidation. Though this is not about legacy BPC I think this might help you (ie see section 4.1 Scenarios).

Hope this helps.

Best regards,

Grégoire

  2. Destination creation in SAP BTP.

entry.title: Re: Released API for F-07 / FB01 / F-44 equivalent postin...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

In this specific case, I asked our internal chatbot for support. Here's the response. Please check whether it helps you resolve the issue:

  1. Outgoing Payment (F-07 Equivalent):

    • Unfortunately, there is no direct API equivalent to F-07 for posting outgoing payments in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The available APIs like API_PAYMENT_INITIATION_SRV are more focused on payment orders rather than direct FI document postings.

  2. Manual GL Document Posting (FB01 Equivalent):

    • The I_JournalEntryTP is indeed the correct business object for posting journal entries, but the CREATE operation is not activated for this entity in the public cloud environment. You might need to explore other options like using the Journal Entry - Post (Synchronous/Asynchronous) APIs for posting GL documents programmatically.

  3. Vendor Special GL Clearing (F-44 Equivalent):

    • There is no direct API for clearing a vendor special GL indicator K item in the public cloud. The I_JournalEntryTP does not support this operation directly.

  4. I_JournalEntryTP Business Object:

    • I_JournalEntryTP is the correct business object for journal entries, but its operations are limited in the public cloud. The CREATE operation is not activated, and this is a restriction in the cloud environment.

  5. Communication Scenario for I_JournalEntryTP:

    • There is no specific communication scenario that needs to be set up for I_JournalEntryTP to work via EML. The limitations are due to the cloud environment restrictions.

Additional Guidance

  • For posting journal entries, consider using the Journal Entry - Post (Synchronous/Asynchronous) APIs.
  • For more complex scenarios like vendor special GL clearing, you might need to explore custom development or alternative approaches within the constraints of the cloud environment.

For more information, see APIs for Journal Entries – The Collection(updated July, 2025)

I hope this helps.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: #CoE#TFAL Treatment of Opening Balances After Histori...
entry.content:
Good day @PDIEGO 

Thank you for your questions.

Question: Is this a best‑practice approach for S/4HANA Public Cloud implementations?

Answer: Partly yes, but I would not call the full design best practice end to end. 

Using Historical Balance for prior, purely historical periods is valid. The migrated historical balances are automatically reversed on the predefined migration key date to have zero balance. That means they are for reporting history, not for establishing a carryforward base into the next year.

So, your observation is correct: if you migrate a full fiscal year with Historical Balance, the year end cumulative balance goes back to zero and the next fiscal year starts with zero from that migration stream. That is expected behaviour, not an error.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/acfecece608a4c11a224ca94c8108eca.html?locale=en-US

The more efficient practice would be:

  • For you to use Historical Balance only for closed historical periods you want available for comparison and reporting
  • Use G/L account balance and open line item at the actual cutover point to establish the productive opening position
  • Then continue with normal posting so standard carryforward works from there onward. There is a distinguishing between these objects, which says, historical financial transaction belongs to FI-Historical balance, while opening balances and open item are handled with FI-G/L account balance and open/line item.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/DATAMIG_S4HANA/0a51c1b7a2e14c1ab54ff8115ff110a1/af8751b510ca4550aeb1ed503eafa50e.html?locale=en-US

That means your FY2026 transition year approach is the part that aligns better with best practice. Once you load the productive opening base with G/L balances and open items and then post normally, standard balance carryforward takes over.

So, my recommendation is:

Your approach is acceptable as a recovery / transition design, but the cleaner best practice is usually to avoid using Historical Balance for an entire year that you expect to feed opening balances into the next year. Instead, keep Historical Balance for legacy history only and establish the operational start position with G/L balances and open items at cutover.

Consult these references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/acfecece608a4c11a224ca94c8108eca.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/fe27b4c6a02546d98bece37b89712d00.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/49c2d553088f4308e10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Exposing BAPI as OData API using RAP Facade
entry.content:
Hi @TulasiDeepthi_L ,

BAPIs calling other BAPIs inside happens often and is no problem.

But a BAPI should never call for a SAP GUI Dialog, since there is nobody to see it in a background service or web service. SAP is usually also testing all BAPIs to make sure this does not happen.

Most likely, a custom BADI implementatio or extension is trying to request additional details or confirmation.

Please look at the dump in transaction "ST22" and scroll to section "call stack" or "active calls" to see which program is starting the dialog. Go up the levels and check for any custom code or extension code.

If found, improve it to check for sy-batch or use module "RFC_IS_GUI_ON" to verfify a human end user is operating the system.  Only call the SAP GUI dialog in that case.

You will also need to think about , what to do if there is no user and implement a default behavior e.g. raise an error message or set a default value.

BR, Marcel

entry.title: Join the Espresso Session: SAP Engagement Cloud - Personalized Experiences for Modern Utilities
entry.content:
Modern utilities companies face a rapidly evolving landscape: rising customer expectations, pressure for transparent communication, and the need to deliver timely, personalized interactions at scale. In this session, we will explore how SAP Engagement Cloud,  formerly known as SAP Emarsys, helps utilities master these challenges with intelligent, automated, and customer‑centric communication.

 

 

Why SAP Engagement Cloud Matters for Utilities

Utility providers increasingly recognize the importance of consistent, personalized engagement, whether informing customers about tariff changes, promoting new services, or sending proactive consumption updates.

SAP Engagement Cloud empowers teams to deliver these experiences with precision, automation, and enterprise-grade scalability.

This session will give you a practical look at how utilities can use SAP’s Customer Experience portfolio to modernize communication across all key customer touchpoints.

What You’ll Learn in This Session

  • Solution Overview: Explore the core capabilities of SAP Engagement Cloud including segmentation, predictive insights, and automated journeys.

  • Live Demo: See how to build personalized campaigns and consumption‑based triggers directly within SAP Engagement Cloud.

  • Best Practices & Implementation Tips: Get practical guidance on integration, data setup, and proven ways to achieve quick value.

Who Should Join?

The session is designed for marketing, customer service, CRM, IT, and SAP architecture professionals in the utilities sector who want to modernize communication and increase customer engagement.

 

If you’re looking for actionable ways to improve customer satisfaction, automate communication, and drive loyalty, this session is for you.

Please register for the session on March 25, 2026 at 4 pm here. 

See you there!

entry.title: Re: Required field GL_ACCOUNT was not transferred in parameter ACCOUNTGL
entry.content: Required field GL_ACCOUNT was not transferred in parameter ACCOUNTGL Message No. RW602 Diagnosis Field GL_ACCOUNT is required in parameter ACCOUNTGL for processing the document. The document is incomplete without this field. System Response The document cannot be processed. Procedure Enter a valid value in field GL_ACCOUNT in parameter ACCOUNTGL.

entry.title: Re: What is the future of SAP ERP in the cloud era?
entry.content:
Hello @Suheb 

First of all, the line between "traditional servers" and "cloud technology" is already thin and very blurred as companies tend to host their applications, even bespoke ones on servers rented from hyperscalers. 

If you ask about SaaS offerings like S/4 public cloud, I think it will go like this (tongue in cheek). First, companies will rush to public cloud because it is fancy, fashionable and it looks cheaper. Then they will struggle with the reality. Then they will realize they pay more for less. I'm pretty sure the "cloud area" will follow the enshittification cycle. 

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: STO Process Intercompany After Outbound Delivery Dire...
entry.content:
Hello @Ashish13 

Thank you for your questions.

If your requirement is an intercompany STO in Public Cloud, then no, the standard Public Cloud process does not support skipping the billing document and continuing with the normal intercompany invoice verification flow.

Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer includes:

  • Processing purchase orders
  • Processing deliveries
  • Billing with intercompany customer invoice
  • Invoice verification based on intercompany supplier invoice.

Consult these references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/136b810e1bf44faa991bd6b7bae14c50.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/db81ae38a02d46fcbe6517ee266ede62.html?locale=en-US

KBA 3689022 also confirms the supplier invoice creation in 5HP is designed to be automatic and the process is built that way for compliance and reconciliation.

So, if your customer wants STO, outbound delivery, freight or transportation charges, GR, MIRO, but without billing document, then that is not the standard supported intercompany process in Public Cloud. The reason is 5HP, MIRO follows the intercompany supplier invoice, and that supplier invoice is tied to the intercompany billing flow.

There is a possibility to handle it if this is a normal stock transfer with delivery and not intercompany value chain, you can run the stock transfer logistics flow with delivery relevance. The Public Cloud includes Stock Transfer with Delivery.

You can also manage transportation charges separately through Transportation Management. Freight orders and freight bookings support:

  • Charge calculation,
  • Freight cost confirmation and posting,
  • Freight invoicing from carrier or service provider invoices.

If the scenario is Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer, you cannot simply remove billing from the process and still expect the standard intercompany supplier invoice and MIRO chain to work. Billing and supplier invoice verification are part of the delivered 5HP design.

Based on the details you provided, there are really two business requirements mixed together:

Option A: customer wants only stock movement plus freight visibility, no intercompany invoicing

Then use a stock transfer with delivery style process and handle freight charges separately in TM. In that case, do not expect a standard MIRO against intercompany supplier invoice, because that belongs to the intercompany scenario.

Option B: Customer wants intercompany STO with GR and MIRO

Then use 5HP Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer, but in this case, billing remains mandatory in the standard process.

 

Because you explicitly mentioned MIRO, the process sounds like you expect an intercompany invoice verification step. In Public Cloud, that points to 5HP, and for that scenario the answer is:

  • Outbound delivery: Yes
  • Transport charges: Yes, via TM if it is in scope
  • Direct GR: Yes
  • MIRO without billing: No, because itis not in the standard intercompany process.

Consult these references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/136b810e1bf44faa991bd6b7bae14c50.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/db81ae38a02d46fcbe6517ee266ede62.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Build Partner Add-on with IDE Action
entry.content:
Disclaimer: The namespace /ITAPC3/ is used throughout this document as an example namespace only. Replace it with your own namespace in real implementations. The coding style/naming conventions used in this blog are not recommendations on naming/design 
  
This blog post covers a step-by-step guide to how partners implement IDE Actions as Partner Add-ons in the SAP BTP ABAP Environment. This enables SAP partners to deliver customized tools in the form of custom action in ABAP Development Tools that increase developer productivity. By combining IDE Actions with the installable add-on option of the BTP ABAP Environment, partners have a potentially monetizable option to extend the ABAP Developer tools. It covers the key concepts and technologies – how to extend ABAP Development Tools with IDE Actions and how to leverage the SAP Business Technology Platform ABAP Environment alongside the Landscape Portal to deliver installable add-on products. 

Here we will look into a partner persona, where they can provide the business solution to their consumer with an installable add-on by providing certain action in the ABAP development tools, and also look into the consumer side of how they can consume the solution which caters to the needs of their developer using ABAP development tools (ADT). 

Why IDE actions and Installable add-ons? 

By combining IDE actions and installable add-ons, partners who have been extending software can now also extend the ABAP Development Tools (ADT). 

  • The IDE action framework allows extending the ABAP development tools (ADT) with custom server-driven context-aware actions. IDE Actions allow partners to automate repetitive tasks, such as creating specialized development objects, enhancing code analysis, or automating some code generation within ABAP Development Tools (ADT) for Eclipse. The implementation is completely server-driven using ABAP only. You can also use these custom functionalities with AI integration and XCO libraries. 
  • Installable add-ons are a set of software components bundled together as an add-on by a partner with the intent of providing a solution to their customers.  
  • The Landscape Portal acts as a central tool to allow service providers to perform administration tasks such as hibernating systems, creating test tenants and support users, selecting systems for a pre-upgrade, but also lifecycle management operations such as building, deploying and updating product versions, and even guides you through the configuration and deployment of multitenant SaaS solutions. 

 Business scenario: 

The developer can document development objects in the Knowledge Transfer Document Editor using Markdown syntax. Here partner is offering a translation action with the help of IDE Action as Add-on for the Knowledge Transfer Document and can use the ABAP AI SDK powered by Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) to translate the Markdown syntax and display the output of translated Knowledge Transfer Document. Consumer (The partner's customer) can consume this solution from the partner through landscape portal as Installable add-on and can use customized action in ABAP Development Tools for their development scenario. 

Prerequisites: (Partner) 

  • You’ve purchased entitlements that are necessary for the account setup. See Prepare 
  • You’ve registered a namespace at SAP, for example /NAMESPC/. See Register a Namespace 
  • You’ve registered your add-on at SAP, for example /NAMESPC/PRODUCTX. See Register a Product 
  • You are subscribed to the Landscape Portal 
  • You have a user with the Landscape Portal Admin role collection assigned 
  • Install ABAP development tools for Eclipse (ADT) on your computer. If you already have ADT, make sure it's updated to the latest version. For more information, see ABAP Development Tools. 
  • You need to have access to an SAP BTP ABAP Environment Instance 
  • Business Catalog SAP_A4C_BC_DEV_AIA_PC required for creating and editing IDE actions 

 Prerequisites: (Consumer) 

  • The Landscape Portal Application has been subscribed to and your user has the landscape portal admin role collection assigned 
  • Install ABAP development tools for Eclipse (ADT) on your computer. If you already have ADT, make sure it's updated to the latest version. For more information, see ABAP Development Tools. 

 Steps to Implement as a Partner 

1. Create Software Components 

  • To open the "Manage Software Components" application, log in to your SAP BTP ABAP Environment Fiori Launchpad as an administrator and Search for "Manage Software Components" in the search bar. 
  • use the “Create” button in the “Manage Software Components” app to open the dialog and fill in the information. We enter the name of the software component and a description. In the namespace dropdown you can use your reserved namespace. (Disclaimer: /ITAPC3/ being an example namespace used in this blog) 

  • You clone the software component into the development system. When cloning, the main branch is checked out for the system, a package is created in the system that has the same name as the software component and is also assigned to it. At the same time, you can also set how the clone of the repository behaves in the system (push/pull or just pull). 
  • The software component with the selected branch is now imported into your current system instance. The Clone button turns into a Pull button. From now on, you can use it to pull (remotely available) changes of your software component to your system. Now you can see package under the selected software component in your system. 

2.  Development (Intelligent Scenario) 

Here in this scenario, we would be using ABAP AI Capabilities is our IDE Action implementation class to translate the markdown content into the target language with the help of ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM. To learn more about Developing AI Enabled Applications with ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM, refer to the blog post. 

2.1 Create ISLM Scenario: 

  • Select your package, right-click, New → Other → Intelligent Scenario → /ITAPC3/ADT_INT_SCE 

Enter a name and description. Select SIDEBYSIDE for Scenario Technology field. Choose Next and then click Finish. 

2.2 Create ISLM Model: 

  • Select your package, right-click, New → Other → Intelligent Scenario Model 
  • Fill the Description and Model Name fields. Assign the previously created scenario in Intelligent Scenario Name. Choose Next.  

  • Enter the ExecutableID and Large Language Model Name and Large Language Model Version and Activate the object. 

3.  Development (IDE Action) 

In this section we will look at the IDE action which translates the markdown content into the selected language with the help of intelligent scenario created in the previous step. 

Partners can utilize the ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM to embed Generative AI features in IDE action implementation class. 

  • From ABAP Development Tools, locate and add to the favorite package “/ITAPC3/IDE_ACTION_EXTENSIONS“ that was created during Create Software Components process. The package has the same name as the software component. 
  • Create a new IDE action either from the main menu, project explorer, or toolbar. 
  • In the main menu, choose File->New->Other... and select IDE Action. The creation wizard opens. 
  • In the creation wizard, enter the project and package details if they are not already filled. Also, provide a name and description for the IDE action. 
  • Choose Next, assign a transport request if needed and Choose Finish. 

  • After we have created the action in the system, we then fill the form with the relevant information to create the action and the Input UI Configuration Class. In the Filter: Cardinality section, decide the number of focused resources that can be selected when running an IDE action. If you want to use your IDE action for any object type, add *ALL as value for the object type. Save and activate your IDE action. 

 

3.1 Defining UI 

  • In the first step we need an action dialog to enable the user to enter data. To do this we can create a new class using the link in the IDE Action. This implements the interface IF_AIA_SD_ACTION_INPUT, but only the method CREATE_INPUT_CONFIG is defined here; the other three methods can be implemented optionally. We define a public type in the class as the structure for the input; we can give the fields certain properties using the ABAP Docs. A list of properties can be found in the documentation, with certain properties being set via configuration. 

 

CLASS /itapc3/cl_trans_ui_info DEFINITION 
  PUBLIC 
  FINAL 
  CREATE PUBLIC . 
  PUBLIC SECTION. 
  INTERFACES if_aia_sd_action_input. 
     "! <p class="shorttext">Simple Controls</p> 
      BEGIN OF ty_input, 
      "! <p class="shorttext">Select the language</p> 
      custom_value_help_text_field TYPE string, 
      "! <p class="shorttext">Enter the markdown source for the translation</p> 
        multiline_text_field         TYPE string, 
      END OF ty_input. 
  PROTECTED SECTION. 
  PRIVATE SECTION. 
ENDCLASS. 

CLASS /ITAPC3/CL_TRANS_UI_INFO IMPLEMENTATION. 
  METHOD if_aia_sd_action_input~create_input_config. 
  " Create the input data with default values 
  DATA(input_data) = VALUE ty_input( ) ##NO_TEXT. 
  " Create the configuration via the factory 
  DATA(configuration) = ui_information_factory>get_configuration_factory( 
                                       )->create_for_data( input_data ). 
  " Set the layout 
  configuration->set_layout( if_sd_config_element=>layout-grid ). 
  " Set configuration for elements 
  “To enable a custom value help for an element, the corresponding configuration must be set via the API: 
   configuration->get_element( 'custom_value_help_text_field' )->set_values( if_sd_config_element=>values_kind-domain_specific_named_items ). 
    configuration->get_element( 'multiline_text_field' )->set_multiline( if_sd_config_element=>height-large ). 
  " Create and return the UI information 
 result = ui_information_factory->for_abap_type( abap_type     = input_data 
             configuration = configuration ). 
 ENDMETHOD. 
 METHOD if_aia_sd_action_input~get_value_help_provider. 
  " Return a reference to your value help handler via the value help provider 
   result = cl_sd_value_help_provider=>create( NEW /ITAPC3/CL_TRAN_VALUE_HELP( ) ). 
  ENDMETHOD. 
ENDCLASS.

 

3.2 Implement a Custom Value Help: 

  • To implement a custom value help that returns domain-specific named items, a handler class must be provided that implements the if_sd_value_help_dsni interface. The get_value_help_items method returns the values for the value help. The values to be returned are of type if_sd_value_help_dsni=>ty_named_item_list. 

 

CLASS /itapc3/cl_trans_ide_action DEFINITION 
  PUBLIC 
  FINAL 
  CREATE PUBLIC. 
  PUBLIC SECTION. 
  INTERFACES if_aia_action. 
  PROTECTED SECTION. 
  PRIVATE SECTION. 
  METHODS get_input_as_html 
      IMPORTING ui_input      TYPE /itapc3/cl_trans_ui_info=>ty_input 
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE string. 
ENDCLASS. 

CLASS /ITAPC3/CL_TRANS_IDE_ACTION IMPLEMENTATION. 
 METHOD get_input_as_html. 
    FINAL(nl) = cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline. 
    TRY. 
        DATA(completion_api) = cl_aic_islm_compl_api_factory=>get( )->create_instance( '/ITAPC3/ADT_INT_SCE' ). 
        DATA(prompt) = |You are a translator. Reject Hate Speech. Translate the given text into { ui_input-custom_value_help_text_field }:{ nl }{ ui_input-multiline_text_field }| ##NO_TEXT. 
        DATA(translation) = completion_api->execute_for_string( prompt )->get_completion( ). 
      CATCH cx_aic_api_factory INTO DATA(aic_api_factory_ex). 
      RETURN. 
      CATCH cx_aic_completion_api INTO DATA(aic_completion_api_ex). 
      RETURN. 
    ENDTRY. 
    result =  '<h3 style="color:gray">Translate to (' && ui_input-custom_value_help_text_field && ')</h3>' && nl  && '<pre>' && translation && '</pre>'. 
  ENDMETHOD. 

  METHOD if_aia_action~run. 
  DATA(focused_resource) = context->get_focused_resource( ). 
    TRY. 
        DATA ui_input TYPE /itapc3/cl_trans_ui_info=>ty_input. 
        context->get_input_config_content( )->get_as_structure( IMPORTING result = ui_input ). 
      CATCH cx_sd_invalid_data INTO DATA(exception). 
        CLEAR ui_input. 
    ENDTRY. 
    DATA(popup_result) = cl_aia_result_factory=>create_html_popup_result( ). 
    popup_result->set_content( get_input_as_html( ui_input ) ). 
    result = popup_result. 
  ENDMETHOD. 
ENDCLASS.

 

To start the action in Eclipse, we can copy the markdown source from any KTD Editor and use  use the shortcut Ctrl + Alt + R for Windows or CMD + OPT + R for Mac or choose Run->ABAP IDE Action... from the main menu to start the Action Dialog, where our IDE Action should now be available. 

Once all the required objects are created and the action is working as per our expectation, we have to release the Transport Request. 

4.  Create Add-on Product using Landscape Portal 

The Landscape Portal acts as a central tool to allow service providers to perform administration tasks such as hibernating systems, creating test tenants and support users, selecting systems for a pre-upgrade, but also lifecycle management operations such as building, deploying and updating product versions, and even guides you through the configuration and deployment of multitenant SaaS solutions. 

Here partner can create a new Add-on product, register your product, build a product version via pipelines and deploy a product version. 

4.1 Register Product 

A product refers to an ABAP add-on that can be installed on SAP BTP, ABAP environment. 

  • Log into the Landscape Portal. For more information, see Landscape Portal   
  • Click on the Register Product tile to open the app. 
  • A list of all your created products, including their product ID, namespace and registration status, is displayed. The registration status indicates whether a product has already been registered to the global account you are currently logged in to. 
  • Click the Create button in the top right corner. 
  • Enter the name of the product you want to create and confirm with Create. Your request for creation has been sent and the registration status of the product changes to “Pending…”. The namespace is automatically determined from the product name you entered, and the product ID is generated as part of the creation process. 

 4.2 Build Product Version 

You can use the Build Product Version app to easily trigger the build of new product versions via pipelines based on templates that you can configure for different use cases such as new (test or production) release deliveries, (test or production) support packages or (test or production) hotfixes/patch deliveries. 

  • Log in to the Landscape Portal and click on the tile Build Product Version. For more information, see Build Product Version 
  • Select one of your products from the list. You will be forwarded to the Product Versions tab. Here, you can see a table listing all the existing versions of this product, their type, when they were created, as well as their delivery status. 
  • You also need to Configure a Pipeline Template for release build. For more information, see Configure a Pipeline Template and Create a template for release build.  
  • To create a new product version, click the Create button, choose Release Delivery and select the desired type. The resulting product version will be calculated automatically. 
  • Select software components to be included in your new product version. To add additional software components, click the Add button on the right. For each software component, define a component version, enter the desired branch and commit ID as seen in the Manage Software Components app, and select in which languages your product version should be built. To get relevant commit ID for the software component involved in the build, you can refer the help page to get more details on this. Identify the commit based on which you wish to build your add-on product. 

  • Click the Build Product Version button to trigger the build of the pipeline for your new product version. 
  • You will be redirected to the Pipeline Status screen, where you can track the progress of your build. 

4.3 Check Product Version 

The Check Product Version app lets you view the results of product version delivery infrastructure checks to see if your product version is ready for delivery.  

  • Click on the Check Product Version tile to open the app. For more information, see Check Product Version 
  • A list of all your products is displayed. Select one of the products from the list. 
  • Ready for production (P): The product version is ready for production use. 

4.4 Publish Product 

You, as a partner, have developed a product that you want to make available to your customers as an add-on that they can install into their system. You can use the Publish Product app to publish your product in a specific version for use by customers. Your customer can then use the Discover Product app to view all product versions that were published, i.e. made available, for them and can register a product, thus adding a product to their pool of product versions available for deployment. 

  1. Log into the Landscape Portal. 
  1. Click the Publish Product tile to open the app. For more information, see Publish Product 
  1. Select the product from the list. You are forwarded to the configuration page. Click the Edit button. 
  1. In the Product Configuration section, select the Product Version that you want to publish and choose SAP Store as the Distribution Channel. 
  1. Click the Publish button to publish your product version 

 

Steps performed from the Consumer 

1. Discover Product 

  • Once Partner has made their product available and has published a product version using the Publish Product app, customers can register the product via Discover Product app. 
  • After registrations customers can deploy the product using Deploy Product.  

2.  Deploy Product 

You can use the Deploy Product app to deploy or update your product version in specific systems. 

  • Sign in to the Landscape Portal. 
  • Click on the Deploy Product tile to open the app. 
  • On the left side, under Product and Product Version Selection, select which product and version you want to deploy or update from the drop-down menu. 
  • In the Available Systems section, select at least one system in which you want to deploy or update the product version. You can also select multiple systems at once. 
  • Click the Deploy button. A dialog box will open. Fill in the remaining details: 
  • Title: Choose a title for your update. 
  • From: Select the time the update can begin. 
  • To: The estimated time at which the product version update will be completed. 
  • Confirm with Schedule. 
  • Check the list under Product Version Update Status. Here, all update requests as well as their current status and progress are displayed. The update you just scheduled has been added to the list. 
  • Once the product is deployed successfully then Customer can access the product in target system. 

SAP BTP automatically activates the software component provided from the partner in the ABAP environment, makes packages visible and registers IDE extensions. 

3.  Test IDE Action 

Create/Use ABAP Cloud project in the ABAP instance where you have deployed the product.This should be a development system. 

Open any Knowledge Transfer Document in the editor and copy the content. 

To open the Run ABAP IDE Action dialog, use the shortcut Ctrl + Alt + R for Windows or CMD + OPT + R for Mac or choose Run->ABAP IDE Action... from the main menu. 

Select the IDE action “Translate markdown content” you would like to run Select Run. 

It will open the action input dialog where you can select the target language you want to translate and in next text box area provide the Knowledge Transfer Document content. 

Select Run. 

The result dialog for the selected IDE action opens the result dialog where it translates the Knowledge Transfer Document to the selected language option from the input dialog.

entry.title: Re: Custom Attributes from Job Role not appearing in Job...
entry.content:
Dear @Athiseshan16 

Good question.

Unfortunately, it is not possible to add custom content in the Job Profile Template. The mapped custom attributes that you can set up on the Job Role will not display in Competency/Skill section in the Job Profile since these are different attribute types. 

There's a KBA that confirms the Job Profile templates only support some default sections 

2560641 - Unable to add Custom Content types in the Job Profile Template
https://me.sap.com/notes/2560641/E

There's an enhancement request available to vote/comment and refers to this topic:

Customized fields/sections in job profile template
 https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/355800/

Hope this will help to clarify, 

Regards, 

Jordi

entry.title: Putaway and Stock Removal Strategies in SAP Logistics Management
entry.content:
A question that we have been asked multiple times: which putaway and stock removal strategies does SAP Logistics Management currently support? And where do I find the relevant configuration / setup?

In this blog post, I would like to share some insights into the current system behaviour: 

The answer to the question is short: SAP Logistics Management currently supports the fixed bin strategy combined with strict FIFO (first in first out) in the outbound process, in inbound it is fixed bin. So what does this mean precisely for putaway and stock removal and how to set it up?

 

Inbound - Putaway Strategy

In the inbound process, when creating a putaway warehouse task, the logistics clerk has two options: manual or automatic task creation.

While in the manual task creation, the logistics clerk can manually enter the destination bin (or leave it empty), the automatic task creation follows this logic:

  1. It checks for a fixed bin assignment of the product. In case this exists, then the fixed bin is taken as destination bin. 
  2. In case it does not find a fixed bin, the destination bin of the task is left empty and has to be entered upon task confirmation by the warehouse operative. 

Both manual and automatic task creation work without fixed bin assignments. 

 

Outbound - Stock Removal Strategy

In the outbound process, the logistics clerk also has two options: manual and automatic warehouse task creation.

Same as in the inbound process, the logistics clerk can manually select the source storage bin during manual task creation: 

The search help on the source storage bin shows a list of unassigned stock:

The second option, automatic task creation, follows this logic: 

  1. It checks for a fixed bin assignment of the product. In case this exists, then the stock is allocated from the fixed bin. 
  2. In case there are multiple or no fixed bins assigned to the product, it follows strict FIFO. 

Also in the outbound process, both manual and automatic task creation work without fixed bin assignments. This enables you to run your processes without setting up anything here and fine-tuning them as you go (the "grow as you go" principle we are following with SAP Logistics Management). 

 

Setup of Fixed bin assignment

The assignment of products to fixed bins happens via the app Manage Fixed Bin Assignments which is part of the app Manage Warehouse Layout :

In this app you can assign one or multiple products to one or multiple fixed bins. 

 

Configure Staging and Goods Receipt bin

There is one prerequisite for the automatic warehouse task creation which is the maintenance of the goods receipt bin in inbound and the staging bin in outbound. You can either enter this manually or configure a default goods receipt and staging bin per warehouse in order to increase the level of automation here.  This configuration happens via the app configure warehouse processes:

 

Conclusion

Even without any configuration or setup, the system will create putaway and stock removal warehouse tasks with the create task automatically functionality. In case you are working with fixed bins, this has to be setup in the above mentioned app (stay tuned for the upload of fixed bin assignments). 

You may argue now: how about other strategies such as FEFO (first expired first out) or empty bin allocation during putaway? The above mentioned is just the beginning so stay tuned and watch out for more and if you like, comment this blog post with the three most relevant putaway and stock removal strategies that you think we should address next.

 

Want to learn more about SAP Logistics Management?

Explore the SAP Logistics Management Online Course 

Visit the SAP Logistics Management Product Page

Have a look at our Roadmap

entry.title: Ask Me Anything with SAP Champions – Monthly Series (Session 2)
entry.content:
Hey dear SAP Community,

My name is Maria Antonieta Lopez,  I currently work as an SAP program director focusing on global SAP transformation programs, SAP S/4HANA implementations, enterprise architecture, governance frameworks, and digital innovation within the SAP ecosystem.

As an SAP Champion myself, I’ll be leading the March edition for the Ask Me Anything series, which we started together with my dear friend Sebastiano Marchesini.

So yes…

You can ask. Me. ANYTHING.

After 20+ years living inside SAP transformations, I’ve probably seen the good, the bad… and the “how did this system survive go-live?” moments. 😅

So over the next weeks I’ll be answering any question you have -- No filters - Just real SAP Live --.

💡We can explore topics like:

⚙️SAP S/4HANA implementations and global transformation programs. - How to successfully deliver end-to-end SAP S/4HANA implementations and large-scale transformation programs, enabling organizations to modernize their core business processes, standardize global operations, and adopt next-generation digital capabilities.

🏗️ SAP Enterprise Architecture. - Processes, artifacts, and strategies for TOGAF-based SAP enterprise architecture.

🧼 Clean Core in SAP Cloud ERP (Private & Public). - Project management processes for implementing or creating extensions or interfaces in your project. (BUILD for configurators and its use with Joule, pre-built APIs, newly developed APIs, and, of course, our platform-as-a-service, SAP Integration Suite, obviously within our beloved SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

☁️ RISE or GROW with SAP + Clean Core Governance & Methodologies. - From SAP Activate  to frameworks such as SAP Application Extension Methodology, Integration Solution Advisory Methodology, SAP Design Thinking, SAP Data Architecture Framework, and why not… even the SAP Business AI Framework.

We can talk about which format to use, or the process for something, or even… why I have to do this task.... and I'll tell you what will happen if you don't...

Muahaha! …💀 soo Yes… my SAP horror stories - like winter - are coming…

🏛️ How to build an SAP Center of Excellence (CoE) or provide an SAP AM services and actually 💙 live happily ever after with SAP 💙 using Cloud ALM + Focused Build + Activate.

🌍Globalization in a nutshell or by country. To comply locally and succeed globally... Long live SAP Document & Reporting Compliance! And our SAP partners' DRC extensions.

🧭 Solution roadmaps, etc.

And... if I don't know the answer? 👉 I promise I'll find someone who does... for you.

 

We have everything ready...  💬 Now it’s your turn.

Drop your question in the comments section 👇or You can ask anonymously here 👉 AMA Champion Anonymously Questions  or Scanning this QR 

 

This AMA edition will be open for questions until March 27, 2026.

After that, selected questions will be answered in a recorded video published on the SAP Community YouTube channel  👉https://www.youtube.com/@SAPCommunity 
A blog recap summarizing the questions and answers will also be published for the community.

Cuentame…

🤓What SAP topic should we explore together? ESPAÑOL or ENGLISH

entry.title: Getting Started with SAP BTP Kyma Runtime Using the CLI
entry.content:
Introduction

If you're learning SAP BTP Kyma Runtime, most tutorials point you straight to the cockpit UI. But understanding the CLI tools — btp and kubectl — gives you a much deeper understanding of what's actually happening under the hood. In this post I'll walk through connecting to a Kyma cluster and exploring its core components purely from the command line.

Prerequisites

  • SAP BTP Trial account with Kyma Runtime enabled
  • btp CLI installed (download here)
  • kubectl installed

Step 1: Find Your Subaccount and Kyma Instance

Log in to BTP via CLI and find your subaccount:

btp login
btp list accounts/subaccount

Then find your Kyma environment instance:

btp list accounts/environment-instance --subaccount <subaccount-id>

You should see something like:

environment name              environment id       environment type   state
<subdomain>-7tn04i0d          <environment-id>     kyma               OK

Get the details including your kubeconfig URL:

btp get accounts/environment-instance <environment-id> --subaccount <subaccount-id>

This returns a labels field containing your KubeconfigURL and APIServerURL.

Step 2: Download the Kubeconfig and Connect kubectl

Download the kubeconfig from the Kyma Dashboard or via the KubeconfigURL, then point kubectl at it:

cp ~/Downloads/kubeconfig.yaml ~/.kube/kyma-config.yaml
export KUBECONFIG=~/.kube/kyma-config.yaml
kubectl get nodes

On a trial cluster you'll see an AWS EC2 worker node:

NAME                        STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION
<node-name>.ec2.internal    Ready    worker   7d    v1.33.7

Step 3: Explore the Kyma Cluster

Namespaces

kubectl get namespaces

Key namespaces:

Namespace Purpose

kyma-systemCore Kyma components
istio-systemService mesh
defaultYour workloads

Core Components in kyma-system

kubectl get pods -n kyma-system

Pod Purpose

api-gateway-controller-managerManages APIRule resources
btp-manager-controller-managerManages BTP service bindings
istio-controller-managerManages Istio service mesh
sap-btp-operator-controller-managerProvisions BTP services as Kubernetes resources
warden-*Image trust validation

Kyma Custom Resources (CRDs)

kubectl api-resources | grep kyma

Key CRDs:

Resource Purpose

APIRuleExpose services externally with auth/routing
RateLimitThrottle API traffic
BtpOperatorConfigure BTP service operator
KymaTop-level Kyma installation resource

Step 4: Understanding an APIRule

The APIRule is the most important Kyma concept for exposing workloads. Here's what a typical one looks like:

apiVersion: gateway.kyma-project.io/v2
kind: APIRule
spec:
  gateway: kyma-system/kyma-gateway
  hosts:
    - myapp.<cluster-id>.kyma.ondemand.com
  rules:
    - methods: [GET, POST]
      noAuth: true
      path: /*
  service:
    name: myapp
    port: 80

Traffic flow:

Internet
  → https://myapp.<cluster-id>.kyma.ondemand.com
  → kyma-gateway (Istio ingress in kyma-system)
  → Service: myapp:80
  → Pod (with Istio sidecar injected automatically)

Note the noAuth: true setting — fine for development but in production you'd replace this with a JWT handler pointing to XSUAA or another identity provider.

Key Observations

  • Istio sidecar injection is automatic — any pod in a labelled namespace gets a second container (2/2 in kubectl get pods)
  • APIRules replace Ingress in Kyma — don't use standard Kubernetes Ingress resources
  • BTP services bind as Kubernetes Secrets — via ServiceInstance and ServiceBinding resources managed by the SAP BTP Operator

Next Steps

  • Bind a BTP service (XSUAA, Destination Service) using ServiceInstance and ServiceBinding
  • Secure an APIRule with JWT authentication
  • Deploy a Kyma Serverless Function
